description_long: This is a leafless deciduous tree with a moderately thick, straight trunk and several main branches that diverge upward and outward. The branches are slender and mostly bare, with only a few small yellowish leaves or buds visible at the tips, suggesting early spring or late autumn. The bark appears smooth to slightly textured, colored in shades of gray and brown. The branching pattern is open and symmetrical, with branches spreading in a V-shape from the trunk. The tree's overall silhouette is airy and sparse, allowing significant visibility through the canopy. No roots are visible above the ground, and the tree stands alone without any surrounding vegetation.
description: A sparse, leafless deciduous tree with a straight trunk and widely spread, slender branches, showing a few yellowish buds at the tips.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical V-shaped branching and the majority of visible buds concentrated on the right branches.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the trunk and the forward-reaching branches, with fewer visible buds.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open structure and even distribution of branches, with minimal foliage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the leftward spread of branches and a clear view of the trunk's base.
